task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #485: feat: Morpheus Phase 2 — Neo fix iteration loop + cooldown ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/clawd/slideheroes-internal-tools` 2. `git fetch origin && git checkout origin/main && git checkout -b sophie/issue-485` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 485 --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run the project's lint/typecheck commands before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat: Morpheus Phase 2 — Neo fix iteration loop + cooldown (#485)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-485` 8. Open PR: ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/slideheroes-internal-tools \ --base main \ --head sophie/issue-485 \ --title "feat: feat: Morpheus Phase 2 — Neo fix iteration loop + cooldown" \ --body "Closes #485 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Do NOT push directly to main. Always work on the feature branch. - Do NOT merge the PR yourself. Just open it and push to the feature branch. - The repo auto-deploys when PRs are merged — you don't need to do anything after opening the PR. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Fix three foundation bugs (cooldown camelCase keys, `task_key` template expansion, `poll_review_comments` exclude_labels filtering, `morpheus_review` NameError) as prerequisites for the feature to function - Update the existing review-response workflow to include Morpheus in its `respondTo` list while fixing broken config keys, enabling initial detection of Morpheus comments - Create a dedicated `morpheus-re-review` workflow with strict one-shot cooldown (key `morpheus:{repo}#{pr_number}`, max 1 attempt) that handles the re-review cycle after Neo fixes, with automatic escalation to Mike via `needs-mike-review` label and comment when the second review still requests changes - Register the new workflow under `can_auto_respond_reviews` and guard sophie-review from Morpheus-blocked PRs to prevent conflicts
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Should the morpheus-triggered fix use the existing review-response workflow or a separate workflow? **Options Considered:** 1. Modify review-response to handle all reviewers including Morpheus, with Morpheus-specific cooldown logic embedded 2. Keep review-response for CodeRabbit/human comments and create a separate morpheus-re-review workflow for the one-shot fix iteration and escalation **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Create a separate workflow for clarity and to enforce the strict one-shot semantics. The review-response workflow adds Morpheus to its respondTo list for initial detection, while a dedicated morpheus-re-review workflow handles the re-review cycle with its own cooldown key and escalation path. This separation makes the hard-stop guarantee easier to reason about and maintain.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Foundation Node Fixes This phase addresses prerequisite bugs in the condition and trigger nodes that would otherwise prevent cooldown tracking and label filtering from working correctly. These fixes are essential before the workflow changes can function.
Task 1: Fix cooldown condition node to accept camelCase keys and expand task_key templates The `not_in_cooldown.py` condition only reads snake_case config keys (`max_attempts`, `dedupWindowMin`) but all workflow JSONs pass camelCase (`maxAttempts`, `dedupWindowMin`). Additionally, `task_key` from config is never template-expanded, so dynamic keys like `morpheus:{{repo}}#{{pr.number}}` would be stored literally. - In `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/conditions/not_in_cooldown.py`, modify the config reading logic (around lines 46-48) to add camelCase fallbacks using `or config.get("maxAttempts", 3)` pattern - Import `expand_template` from `nodes.utils` and apply it to `task_key` immediately after resolution, checking for `{{` presence before expanding - This enables workflow configs to use either naming convention and allows dynamic cooldown keys scoped to specific PRs
Task 2: Fix poll_github_prs to honor excludeLabels in review_comments mode The `_poll_review_comments` method does not receive or filter by `exclude_labels`, unlike `_poll_prs`. This means PRs with blocking labels (like `morpheus:re-reviewed`) cannot be excluded from review-comment polling. - In `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/triggers/poll_github_prs.py`, update the call site in `execute()` (around line 65) to pass `exclude_labels` to `_poll_review_comments` - Update `_poll_review_comments` signature (around line 279) to accept `exclude_labels` parameter with default `None` - Add label-filtering logic before appending PRs, mirroring the pattern used in `_poll_prs` (lines 129-134)
Task 3: Fix NameError in morpheus_review build_prompt method The `_build_prompt` method references bare `config` which is not in scope, causing a runtime NameError. The method should use `self._config` which is already set in `execute()`. - In `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/actions/morpheus_review.py`, locate line 325 in `_build_prompt` and change `config.get("prompt_template_path", ...)` to `self._config.get("prompt_template_path", ...)` - This aligns with the pattern already used in `_check_skip_conditions` and `_apply_skip_label` methods
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase fixes three prerequisite bugs in condition and trigger nodes. The goal is to ensure cooldown tracking, label filtering, and morpheus_review prompt building all work correctly before any workflow changes are applied. **Task 1: Fix `not_in_cooldown.py` camelCase key support and template expansion** - File: `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/conditions/not_in_cooldown.py` - Around lines 46-48, update config reading to support camelCase fallbacks: - `max_attempts`: also try `config.get("maxAttempts", 3)` - `dedup_window_min`: also try `config.get("dedupWindowMin", ...)` - Import `expand_template` from `nodes.utils` - After resolving `task_key`, check if it contains `{{` and if so, apply `expand_template` to it **Task 2: Fix `poll_github_prs.py` to support `excludeLabels` in review_comments mode** - File: `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/triggers/poll_github_prs.py` - In `execute()` (around line 65), pass `exclude_labels` when calling `_poll_review_comments` - Update the `_poll_review_comments` method signature (around line 279) to accept `exclude_labels=None` - Add label-filtering logic inside `_poll_review_comments` before appending PRs, mirroring the existing pattern in `_poll_prs` (lines 129-134) **Task 3: Fix `morpheus_review.py` NameError in `_build_prompt`** - File: `scripts/neo-loop/nodes/actions/morpheus_review.py` - On line 325, change `config.get("prompt_template_path", ...)` to `self._config.get("prompt_template_path", ...) ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
label: neo-issue-slideheroes-internal-tools-485
